mnemonics.title: Green IT Pillars
mnemonics.content: Remember the three pillars using: 'EES' = Economic, Environmental, Social - like an 'E' for Ecology and 'E' for Economy working 'S'ustainably together

mnemonics.title: Key Areas of Green IT
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'MUDA': Manufacturing, Use, Disposal, Analytics (Design)

mnemonics.title: E-Waste Hazards
mnemonics.content: Remember toxic metals using: 'Lead (Pb) Makes Children Sick' = Lead, Mercury, Cadmium, Chromium

acronyms.usage: Metric to measure data center energy efficiency - lower is better, ideally close to 1.0
Discarded electronic devices requiring special disposal due to hazardous materials
Practice of environmentally sustainable computing and IT operations
Includes purchase cost plus operational and disposal costs, important for Green IT decisions
Reciprocal of PUE; measures percentage of power used by IT equipment

visualTips.visualization: Picture a funnel: Total power enters at top, splits into IT equipment power and facility overhead (cooling, lighting), with efficiency measured by how much reaches the IT equipment
Picture a circle: Design → Manufacturing → Use → Disposal → Recycling → Back to Design (circular economy)
Picture a multi-story building (physical server) with many apartments (virtual machines), each using shared resources like elevator (network) and utilities

analogies.analogy: Like a apartment building - one physical structure houses multiple independent living spaces, each appearing separate while sharing common infrastructure
Like dumping toxic chemicals in a river - the harmful substances persist and contaminate, affecting ecosystem health
Like carpooling - instead of everyone driving separate cars (individual servers), multiple passengers (users) share one vehicle (cloud resources), reducing total fuel (energy) consumption
